flashcards.back: A subgroup is a subset H of a group G that itself forms a group under the same binary operation as G. It must contain the identity element, be closed under the operation, and contain inverses for all its elements.
If H is a non-empty subset of group G, then H is a subgroup of G if for all a,b ∈ H, a*b⁻¹ ∈ H. This single condition ensures closure under inverses and the operation.
If H is a non-empty subset of group G, then H is a subgroup of G if: (1) for all a,b ∈ H, ab ∈ H (closure), and (2) for all a ∈ H, a⁻¹ ∈ H (inverses). The identity element is automatically included.
The trivial subgroup is {e}, containing only the identity element of the group G. It is a subgroup of every group and is the smallest possible subgroup.
A proper subgroup is a subgroup H of G such that H ≠ G. It is a subset that is strictly smaller than the entire group but still retains the group structure.

The cyclic subgroup generated by an element a ∈ G is ⟨a⟩ = {aⁿ : n ∈ Z}. It is the smallest subgroup of G containing a, and consists of all powers of a (positive, negative, and zero power which gives identity).

flashcards.front: Define the center of a group
flashcards.back: The center Z(G) = {z ∈ G : gz = zg for all g ∈ G} is the set of all elements that commute with every element of G. Z(G) is always a normal subgroup of G.

flashcards.front: Define the order of an element
flashcards.back: The order of an element a in group G is the smallest positive integer n such that aⁿ = e (identity). If no such n exists, the element has infinite order.
For a subgroup H of G and element g ∈ G, the left coset gH = {gh : h ∈ H}. Right coset is Hg = {hg : h ∈ H}. Cosets partition the group G.
If H is a subgroup of a finite group G, then |H| divides |G|. The number of cosets of H in G is |G|/|H|, called the index.

flashcards.front: What is the index of a subgroup?
flashcards.back: The index of H in G, denoted [G:H], is the number of distinct left (or right) cosets of H in G. For finite groups, [G:H] = |G|/|H|.
A non-empty finite subset H of a group G is a subgroup of G if and only if H is closed under the group operation (ab ∈ H for all a,b ∈ H).

flashcards.front: What is a generator of a cyclic group?
flashcards.back: An element a ∈ G is a generator of cyclic group G if ⟨a⟩ = G. A cyclic group may have more than one generator; if G = ⟨a⟩, then a⁻¹ is also a generator.
The commutator subgroup (or derived subgroup) G' = ⟨[a,b] : a,b ∈ G⟩ where [a,b] = aba⁻¹b⁻¹. It is the smallest normal subgroup of G such that G/G' is abelian.

flashcards.front: When is a subgroup called characteristic?
flashcards.back: A subgroup H of G is characteristic if φ(H) = H for every automorphism φ of G. Every characteristic subgroup is normal, but not every normal subgroup is characteristic.
